title: Automata Forge
description: A from-scratch theory-of-computation lab spanning two levels of the Chomsky hierarchy. REGULAR: compile a regex into a parse tree, Thompson e-NFA, subset-construction DFA, Hopcroft-minimized DFA and a Brzozowski-derivative DFA; step strings through any of them; compare two regexes via the product automaton (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference), decide equivalence/containment and prove inequality with the shortest distinguishing string; or draw your own automaton in Build mode and watch the pipeline run live, with a Myhill-Nerode table and direct NFA-to-regex. NEW in v4 — Grammar mode, the context-free laboratory: write a grammar and analyze it (nullable / generating / reachable / useless symbols, FIRST & FOLLOW), normalize it step-by-step to Chomsky Normal Form, recognize strings two ways (the CYK table on CNF and an Earley chart on the raw grammar), render the derivation tree with ambiguity detection, sample the language, compile the grammar to a pushdown automaton and watch its stack accept your string, and play the CFL pumping lemma. A bridge turns any regex into an equivalent right-linear grammar. No libraries — every parser, automaton algorithm, the graph layout and the SVG renderer are hand-written and differential-tested.
